end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 164 actions; a strong score needs about 59 or fewer. You are 2.8x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

After clicking the blue container:
- Blue block is now at rows 34-37, cols 8-11 (back to original position)
- Checkerboard is now at rows 20-23, cols 18-21 (swapped with blue block)
- Red bar is vertical at rows 24-29, cols 18-21
- Green center at rows 38-39, cols 10-11
Looking at the image, I can see:
- The checkerboard (blue pixels) is now at rows 20-23, cols 18-21 (above the red bar)
- The red bar is vertical at rows 24-29, cols 18-21
- The dark red box is at rows 30-33, cols 18-21
- The blue block is at rows 34-37, cols 8-11
- The green center is at rows 38-39, cols 10-11 (inside gray box)
So the current configuration on the left side is:
- Checkerboard (blue) at rows 20-23, cols 18-21
- Red bar (vertical) at rows 24-29, cols 18-21
- Dark red box at rows 30-33, cols 18-21
- Blue block at rows 34-37, cols 8-11
- Green center at rows 38-39, cols 10-11
The right side shows:
- Red container with red bar display
- Blue container with blue block display
Looking at the image more carefully, I notice that the checkerboard is now at the top (rows 20-23, cols 18-21), the red bar is below it (rows 24-29, cols 18-21), and the dark red box is below that (rows 30-33, cols 18-21). This forms a continuous vertical strip.
But wait, the blue block is at rows 34-37, cols 8-11, which is to the left of the dark red box. The green center is at rows 38-39, cols 10-11, inside the gray box at rows 38-43, cols 8-13.
Let me think about what the goal might be. The containers on the right show:
- Red container: shows a red bar (horizontal)
- Blue container: shows a blue block (horizontal)
Maybe the goal is to have the left side match the right side display. The right side shows a horizontal red bar and a horizontal blue block. So the goal might be to have:
- Red bar horizontal (rows 30-33, cols 12-17)
- Blue block in some position
- Green center in some position
But I've already tried clicking the red container to toggle the red bar to horizontal, and the level didn't complete.
Let me try a different approach. Maybe the goal is to move the green center to overlap with the yellow square (rows 20-21, cols 24-25) and then click something.
To move the green center from rows 38-39, cols 10-11 to rows 20-21, cols 24-25:
- UP 18 (to rows 20-21)
- RIGHT 14 (to cols 24-25)
But the green center might be blocked by objects. Let me check the path:
- From rows 38-39, cols 10-11, moving UP would go through rows 34-37 (blue block at cols 8-11), rows 30-33 (gray box at cols 8-11, dark red box at cols 18-21), rows 24-29 (red bar at cols 18-21), rows 20-23 (checkerboard at cols 18-21).
- The green center is at cols 10-11, so moving UP would not be blocked by the red bar (cols 18-21) or checkerboard (cols 18-21).
- But the blue block is at rows 34-37, cols 8-11, which overlaps with cols 10-11. So the green center would be blocked by the blue block when moving UP.
Wait, the green center is at rows 38-39, cols 10-11, and the blue block is at rows 34-37, cols 8-11. The green center is below the blue block, so moving UP would move the green center towards the blue block. If the green center moves UP, it would reach rows 36-37, cols 10-11, which is still below the blue block (rows 34-37). So the green center would be blocked by the blue block.
Actually, looking at the segmentation, the blue block is at rows 34-37, cols 8-11, and the green center is at rows 38-39, cols 10-11. The gray box is at rows 38-43, cols 8-13. So the green center is inside the gray box, and the blue block is above the gray box.
If I move the green center UP, it would move out of the gray box and into the blue block's position. But the blue block is at rows 34-37, cols 8-11, and the green center is at rows 38-39, cols 10-11. Moving UP once would move the green center to rows 36-37, cols 10-11, which overlaps with the blue block (rows 34-37, cols 8-11). This might cause game over or the green center might be blocked.
Let me try moving the green center RIGHT first to avoid the blue block, then UP.
- From rows 38-39, cols 10-11, moving RIGHT would go to cols 12-13, 14-15, etc.
- The gray box is at rows 38-43, cols 8-13, so the green center is inside it. Moving RIGHT would move the green center to the right edge of the gray box.
- After moving RIGHT 2, the green center would be at cols 12-13, which is still inside the gray box.
- After moving RIGHT 3, the green center would be at cols 13-14, which is outside the gray box (cols 8-13).
Actually, I'm not sure if the green center is constrained to stay inside the gray box or if it can move freely. Let me try moving the green center RIGHT to see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current state:
# - Blue block at rows 34-37, cols 8-11
# - Red bar vertical at rows 24-29, cols 18-21
# - Green center at rows 38-39, cols 10-11 (inside gray box)
# - Checkerboard at rows 20-23, cols 18-21

# Try moving green center RIGHT to see if it can exit gray box
action(['RIGHT'])
print("After RIGHT:")
print("  Board changed:", last_action_result.get('board_changed'))
print("  Game over:", last_action_result.get('game_over'))

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        boundary = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in boundary]
        cols = [p[1] for p in boundary]
        print(f"  Green center: rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
After RIGHT:
  Board changed: True
  Game over: False
  Green center: rows=38-39, cols=12-13
